project.name: openclaw-dashboard
project.description: OpenClaw Dashboard is a zero-dependency Go HTTP server with an embedded single-page frontend (web/index.html via go:embed) that provides a local command center for OpenClaw AI agents — surfacing gateway health, costs, cron status, active sessions, sub-agent runs, model usage, and system metrics. It uses only the Go standard library and follows a root-package facade pattern over domain-organized internal/app* packages. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: go-idioms.md
nodes.summary: Reference guide of Go 1.26 modern idioms (new(expr), iterators, omitzero struct tags) and go fix modernizers that Claude must apply when generating code for this project.

nodes.summary: In-process CLI tests that capture stdio and reset flag state to validate version flags and subcommand handling without spawning a subprocess.

nodes.summary: Root-package tests for config loading covering defaults, overrides, invalid JSON, zero-value clamping, dotenv parsing edge cases, and home directory expansion.

nodes.name: config.go
nodes.summary: Root-package facade exposing defaultConfig, loadConfig, readDotenv, and expandHome by delegating to the internal/appconfig package.

nodes.summary: Contributor guide covering quick start, core constraints, test suite overview, change-type testing guidance, security testing, TDD workflow, and PR checklist.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build compiling a CGO-free Go binary in a golang:1.26-alpine builder and running it as a non-root user in an alpine:3.23 runtime image with a healthcheck exposing port 8080.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-stage build with CGO_ENABLED=0 static binary, non-root user 1001, and a wget-based HEALTHCHECK.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: First build stage based on golang:1.26-alpine that copies Go sources and web assets and produces the static openclaw-dashboard binary.

nodes.name: alpine
nodes.summary: Runtime stage based on alpine:3.23 that copies the built binary and runtime assets, runs as a non-root user, and exposes port 8080 with a healthcheck.

nodes.name: CONFIGURATION.md
nodes.summary: Configuration reference documenting every config.json field (bot, theme, timezone, panels, refresh, server, alerts, system metrics, AI chat), environment variables, security notes, and data flow.

nodes.name: graphify.md
nodes.summary: Short note describing the canonical graphify knowledge-graph workflow and rules for the project.

nodes.summary: Documentation explaining how the Homebrew tap is updated, the required release secret, and the runtime layout for Homebrew-installed deployments.

nodes.name: INFRA-CHECKLIST.md

nodes.summary: Historical note on the AI chat integration pointing to the current authoritative files and describing present behavior.

nodes.name: callGateway
nodes.summary: Facade wrapper forwarding AI gateway requests (context, system prompt, history, question, port, token, model, client) to appchat.CallGateway.

nodes.name: buildBinary
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds the openclaw-dashboard binary once via sync.Once into a temp directory for use by the smoke tests.

nodes.name: runBin
nodes.summary: Test helper that runs the built binary with given args and timeout, capturing combined output and exit code with a kill-on-timeout safeguard.

nodes.name: captureStdio
nodes.summary: Test helper that redirects os.Stdout/Stderr through pipes, runs the supplied function, and returns the captured stdout and stderr strings.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Invokes dashboard.Main and returns its exit code to be passed to os.Exit by main.

nodes.name: loadConfig
nodes.summary: Facade wrapper delegating configuration loading for a directory to appconfig.Load.

nodes.summary: Validated roadmap for fixing issues #25 (blank cron last/next run) and #26 (empty dashboard sections), specifying cron sidecar merge, bounded /api/system cold-path, frontend terminal states, and a TDD phase breakdown.

nodes.name: 2026-04-08-service-management.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive task-by-task implementation plan for OS service management (launchd/systemd), covering the backend interface, FormatStatus tests, subcommand dispatch, plist/unit generation, and end-to-end smoke testing.

nodes.name: 2026-04-08-service-management-design.md
nodes.summary: Design specification for the appservice package, defining the CLI surface, status output format, platform-specific backend interface (launchd on macOS, systemd on Linux), and main.go integration.

nodes.name: config.full.json
nodes.summary: Fully-populated example dashboard configuration showing every supported section: bot, theme, timezone, refresh, server, alerts, ai, and system.

nodes.name: config.minimal.json
nodes.summary: Minimal example dashboard configuration containing only the bot section, demonstrating that all other settings fall back to defaults.

nodes.name: flake.nix
nodes.summary: Nix flake defining reproducible build and development environments for the dashboard, building the Go binary with CGO disabled across supported platforms.
nodes.languageNotes: Nix flake with CGO_ENABLED=0 to match the project's pure-Go build constraint.

nodes.name: GEMINI.md
nodes.summary: Minimal pointer document referencing the graphify knowledge-graph workflow.

nodes.name: go.mod
nodes.summary: Go module definition pinning the module path and Go 1.26 toolchain with no third-party dependencies, enforcing the project's zero-dependency stdlib-only constraint.
nodes.languageNotes: No require block / go.sum — zero third-party dependencies by design.

nodes.name: install.sh
nodes.summary: Installer shell script that downloads and sets up the dashboard binary, using a temp-directory cleanup trap for safe extraction.

nodes.name: cleanup_tmp
nodes.summary: Trap handler that removes the temporary working directory created during installation.

nodes.name: chat_redaction_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for CallGateway error handling, verifying no-choices errors, empty-content acceptance, and that bearer tokens are redacted from error messages.

nodes.name: chat_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the appchat package covering BuildSystemPrompt output and CallGateway success, server-error, timeout, and empty-model behaviors against an httptest server.

nodes.name: chat.go
nodes.summary: Core appchat logic: builds the AI system prompt from dashboard metrics and calls the AI gateway over HTTP, with token redaction and structured GatewayError handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses strings.Builder with pre-Grow for efficient prompt assembly; manual JSON marshal/unmarshal stdlib-only.

nodes.name: BuildSystemPrompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the AI system prompt string from dashboard data (tokens, costs, sessions, crons, system metrics) using a pre-sized strings.Builder.

nodes.name: CallGateway
nodes.summary: Sends a chat completion request to the AI gateway over HTTP, handling timeouts, status errors, body limits, and JSON parsing while redacting the bearer token from any error.

nodes.name: redactToken
nodes.summary: Replaces occurrences of the bearer token in a string with a placeholder to keep secrets out of error messages and logs.

nodes.name: GatewayError
nodes.summary: Error type carrying an HTTP status and message, returned by CallGateway so callers can branch on gateway failures via errors.As.

nodes.name: cold_path_cap_test.go
nodes.summary: Test verifying that the system ColdPathTimeoutMs config value is clamped to its allowed bounds during Load.

nodes.name: config_dotenv_export_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for ReadDotenv handling of the `export` prefix, including extra whitespace and cases where `export` is part of the literal key.

nodes.name: config_strict_test.go
nodes.summary: Test verifying Load emits a slog warning when an unknown config field is present, using a captured-slog test helper.

nodes.name: config_test.go
nodes.summary: Comprehensive tests for the appconfig package: Default population, Load JSON/partial/invalid handling, gateway-port inheritance, threshold clamping, ReadDotenv parsing, and ExpandHome tilde expansion.

nodes.name: config.go
nodes.summary: Defines the dashboard configuration structs and loaders: Default values, JSON Load with unknown-key warnings and threshold clamping, .env parsing, and home-directory expansion.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses reflect to walk JSON tags for strict unknown-key detection; stdlib-only config loading.

nodes.name: Default
nodes.summary: Returns a fully-populated Config with sensible defaults for every section, used as the baseline before JSON overrides are applied.

nodes.name: Load
nodes.summary: Loads config.json from a directory onto the defaults, warning on unknown keys and malformed JSON, applying gateway-port inheritance and clamping metric thresholds.

nodes.name: ReadDotenv
nodes.summary: Parses a .env file into a key/value map, handling comments, the `export` prefix, and quoted values.

nodes.name: walkUnknownKeys
nodes.summary: Recursively compares a parsed JSON map against a struct type's JSON tags and warns about any unrecognized configuration keys.

nodes.name: ExpandHome
nodes.summary: Expands a leading tilde in a path to the user's home directory, returning the path unchanged when no tilde is present.

nodes.name: Config
nodes.summary: Top-level dashboard configuration struct aggregating bot, theme, refresh, server, AI, logs, alerts, and system sub-configs.

nodes.name: SystemConfig
nodes.summary: System-metrics configuration: polling/TTL intervals, gateway and cold-path timeouts, disk path, and per-metric warn/critical thresholds.

nodes.name: LogsConfig
nodes.summary: Log-tailing configuration: tail line counts, fast-refresh intervals, error window and signature limits, and log sources.

nodes.name: apprefresh_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the apprefresh collector: token-usage caching with large JSONL lines, unchanged-file summary reuse, live model-lookup caching, resolved openclaw binary usage, session-model resolution, and openclaw config parsing.

nodes.name: bench_test.go
nodes.summary: Benchmarks for hot-path apprefresh routines: token-usage file parsing, daily chart building, cost breakdown, bucket-to-list conversion, and model-name resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Go 1.24+ b.Loop() for benchmark iteration.

nodes.name: cron_state_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for CollectCrons covering sidecar-only, legacy-inline-only, missing job ID, malformed sidecar, sidecar-wins precedence, empty-state fallback, partial override, and last-run status fallback scenarios.

nodes.name: doc_alignment_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests verifying openclaw config parsing aligns with documented behavior: telegram stream mode nested-over-legacy resolution, plugin enabled-state surfacing, and binding account/guild/team carry-through.

nodes.name: logtail_test.go
nodes.summary: Extensive tests for logtail: tail-line reading, multi-source latest-record merging with caps, error-signature normalization, timestamp parsing across layouts/timezones, severity classification, record comparison, and log-path resolution safety.

nodes.name: logtail.go
nodes.summary: Log-tailing engine for the apprefresh collector: tails multiple log sources, merges the latest records via a heap, parses JSONL/plain lines, classifies severity, normalizes error signatures, and resolves log paths safely.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses container/heap for k-way merge of per-source records and compiled regexps for timestamp/error-signature parsing.

nodes.name: ReadMergedLogs
nodes.summary: Reads tail lines from each configured log source, parses them into records, and merges the most recent ones across all sources up to a global limit.

nodes.name: mergeLatestRecords
nodes.summary: Performs a heap-based k-way merge of per-source log records to produce the globally most recent records up to the limit.

nodes.name: parseLogLine
nodes.summary: Parses a single log line, dispatching to JSONL or plain-text parsing based on file extension and applying a fallback timestamp when none is found.

nodes.name: parseJSONLLine
nodes.summary: Parses a JSONL log line into a LogRecord, extracting timestamp, message, and severity, falling back to plain parsing when the JSON is invalid.

nodes.name: parsePlainLine
nodes.summary: Parses a plain-text log line into a LogRecord, extracting a timestamp and gateway-line message and inferring severity.

nodes.name: ParseLogTimestamp
nodes.summary: Parses a log timestamp by trying multiple layouts in local time and falling back to extracting a timestamp from a log-line prefix.

nodes.name: classifySeverity
nodes.summary: Classifies a log line's severity by scanning for severity keyword tokens while avoiding false positives from substrings.

nodes.name: getString
nodes.summary: Coerces a JSON value into a trimmed string, handling string, number, and integer representations.

nodes.name: NormalizeErrorSignature
nodes.summary: Normalizes an error message into a stable signature by lowercasing and stripping UUIDs, ID tokens, numbers, and log prefixes for grouping.

nodes.name: readTailLines
nodes.summary: Reads up to a limited number of trailing lines from a file with a bounded scanner buffer, truncating oversized lines.

nodes.name: ResolveLogPath
nodes.summary: Resolves and sanitizes a log source path against the openclaw base directory, rejecting traversal and absolute escapes.

nodes.name: compareLogRecords
nodes.summary: Orders two log records by timestamp with deterministic tie-breaking on seen-time and source, treating zero timestamps consistently.

nodes.name: LogRecord
nodes.summary: Represents a single parsed log entry with source, timestamps, severity, message, and raw line.

nodes.name: refresh.go
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the dashboard data collection pipeline: fans out concurrent collectors (gateway, crons, git), parses config, aggregates sessions and token usage, and writes data.json atomically.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a WaitGroup to parallelize independent collectors and an atomic write (temp file + rename) for the output.

nodes.name: RunRefreshCollector
nodes.summary: Runs the full collection pipeline and atomically writes the marshaled dashboard data to data.json via temp-file rename.

nodes.name: ModelName
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw model identifier into a human-friendly display name through an ordered chain of substring matchers.

nodes.name: collectDashboardData
nodes.summary: Core collector that concurrently gathers gateway health, crons, git log, sessions, and token usage, then assembles the complete dashboard data structure.

nodes.name: refresh_alerts.go
nodes.summary: Builds dashboard alerts from cost, cron, context, gateway, and memory thresholds, and produces a sorted cost breakdown.

nodes.name: BuildAlerts
nodes.summary: Evaluates cost, cron status, context window, gateway state, and memory thresholds to assemble the list of dashboard alerts.

nodes.name: BuildCostBreakdown
nodes.summary: Converts a model-cost map into a list sorted by descending cost with rounded values.

nodes.name: refresh_chart.go
nodes.summary: Builds the 30-day daily chart series by merging freshly computed daily aggregates with previously persisted chart data from disk.

nodes.name: BuildDailyChart
nodes.summary: Assembles a 30-day window of cost, token, call, and subagent series, merging today's computed values with historical data loaded from the persisted chart file.

nodes.name: refresh_config.go
nodes.summary: Parses the raw OpenClaw config JSON into structured dashboard config: agents, models, channels, hooks, plugins, skills, bindings, and memory/compaction policies.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of dynamic map[string]any JSON traversal via shared helper accessors rather than typed structs.

nodes.name: lookupModelLimits
nodes.summary: Resolves context-window and output limits for a given model ID by searching the available-models config block.

nodes.name: parseMemoryPolicy
nodes.summary: Extracts the memory-flush policy settings from the compaction defaults config block.

nodes.name: parseOpenclawConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level config parser that orchestrates all sub-parsers to build the complete structured dashboard configuration from raw OpenClaw config JSON.

nodes.name: parseChannels
nodes.summary: Parses channel configuration (telegram, discord, etc.) into sorted, structured channel entries with connectivity state.

nodes.name: parseBindings
nodes.summary: Parses agent-to-channel binding rules from config into structured binding entries.

nodes.name: parseAgents
nodes.summary: Parses the agents config array into structured agent entries, resolving model aliases, params, fallbacks, and display names.

nodes.name: refresh_crons.go
nodes.summary: Collects cron job state from the OpenClaw jobs file, supporting both legacy-inline and split-store (sidecar state) layouts.

nodes.name: loadCronStateSidecar
nodes.summary: Loads the split-store cron state sidecar file and indexes per-job runtime state by job ID.

nodes.name: CollectCrons
nodes.summary: Reads the cron jobs file, merges inline or sidecar state, and produces per-job summaries with formatted last/next run times and statuses.

nodes.name: refresh_gateway.go
nodes.summary: Probes AI gateway health by combining an HTTP /healthz check with a pgrep process scan, then enriches with process uptime and RSS via ps.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses injectable function seams (healthzProbe, pgrepGateway) for testability and context.WithTimeout for each external probe.

nodes.name: collectGatewayHealth
nodes.summary: Determines gateway online/offline status from an HTTP healthz probe and a pgrep scan (filtering the self PID), then attaches process uptime and memory usage.

nodes.name: refresh_git.go
nodes.summary: Collects the last five git commits from the OpenClaw repo via a timeout-bounded git log subprocess.

nodes.name: collectGitLog
nodes.summary: Runs git log to fetch and parse the five most recent commits (hash, subject, relative age) for the dashboard.

nodes.name: refresh_helpers.go
nodes.summary: Shared helper utilities for the apprefresh package: typed JSON map accessors, rounding, date filtering, slice limiting, and sorted key iteration.

nodes.name: FilterByDate
nodes.summary: Filters cron run records relative to a target date using a comparison operator (before/after/equal).

nodes.name: refresh_sessions.go
nodes.summary: Collects and enriches session data: loads per-agent session stores, derives group names, resolves session models from JSONL tails, and backfills channel connectivity.

nodes.name: SessionStoreFile
nodes.summary: Struct pairing an agent name with its loaded session store map.

nodes.name: buildGroupNames
nodes.summary: Derives human-readable group/channel display names from session store keys, skipping empty group IDs and extracting subject lines.

nodes.name: enrichBindings
nodes.summary: Fills in human-readable names on agent config bindings using the derived group-name lookup.

nodes.name: readLastSessionModel
nodes.summary: Reads the tail of a session JSONL file and extracts the most recent model name without loading the whole file.

nodes.name: collectSessions
nodes.summary: Builds the enriched session list from loaded stores: resolves models (live and persisted), formats timestamps, trims labels, and sorts by recency.

nodes.name: backfillChannelConnectivity
nodes.summary: Marks agent-config channels as active based on observed session activity.

nodes.name: refresh_tokens.go
nodes.summary: Defines token-usage bucket types and aggregation helpers, formats token counts, and collects per-model token usage across time windows.

nodes.name: TokenBucket
nodes.summary: Accumulator struct holding call count and input/output/cache/total token counts plus cost, with an add method.

nodes.name: TokenUsageEntry
nodes.summary: Serializable per-model token usage entry with both formatted and raw token figures.

nodes.name: BucketsToList
nodes.summary: Converts a model-keyed token bucket map into a cost-sorted list of formatted usage entries.

nodes.name: CollectTokenUsage
nodes.summary: Public entry point that delegates token usage collection across all time windows to the cached implementation.

nodes.name: session_model_cache.go
nodes.summary: TTL-bounded, singleflight cache of live session models fetched from the OpenClaw CLI, with injectable fetch and binary-resolution seams.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses sync.Cond for singleflight coordination so concurrent callers share one in-flight refresh.

nodes.name: liveSessionModelCache
nodes.summary: Mutex- and cond-guarded cache holding the live session-model map, expiry, refreshing flag, and injectable fetch/resolve/runner seams.

nodes.name: fetch
nodes.summary: Returns cached session models when fresh, otherwise coordinates a single in-flight refresh via cond wait/broadcast and updates the cache.

nodes.name: fetchLiveSessionModelsCLI
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenClaw sessions CLI with a timeout and parses its JSON output into a session-ID-to-model map.

nodes.name: jobs-state.lastRunStatusOnly.json
nodes.summary: Cron test fixture providing a split-store state sidecar containing only last-run status entries.

nodes.name: jobs-state.split-store.json
nodes.summary: Cron test fixture providing the split-store runtime state sidecar paired with jobs.split-store.json.

nodes.name: jobs.legacy-inline.json
nodes.summary: Cron test fixture for the legacy layout where job definitions and runtime state are inlined in a single file.

nodes.name: jobs.split-store.json
nodes.summary: Cron test fixture for the split-store layout where job definitions live separately from runtime state.

nodes.name: refresh_alerts_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for BuildAlerts threshold logic, cost breakdown sorting, date filtering, slice limiting, and the frozen daily-chart merge contract.

nodes.name: refresh_config_limits_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests model-limit lookup, memory-policy parsing, and that config parsing surfaces primary model limits.

nodes.name: refresh_crons_agentid_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests that CollectCrons surfaces job ID and agent ID, and falls back to an empty string when agent ID is missing.

nodes.name: refresh_gateway_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests gateway health detection across pgrep/HTTP combinations, self-PID filtering, port-zero skip, and port-field population using stub seams.

nodes.name: refresh_modelname_test.go
nodes.summary: Table-driven test verifying ModelName ordering and boundary cases for o1/o3 model identifiers.

nodes.name: refresh_sessions_gid_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests that buildGroupNames skips entries with an empty group ID.

nodes.name: refresh_sessions_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests session group-name extraction, binding enrichment, and channel-connectivity backfill.

nodes.name: session_model_cache_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests cache instance isolation and singleflight behavior, asserting concurrent fetches trigger only one underlying refresh.

nodes.name: session_model_reader_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests JSONL line model extraction and tail-reading of session model files across small, large, and edge-case file layouts.

nodes.name: token_usage_cache_mode_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit test verifying the token usage cache file is persisted with 0o600 permissions for security.

nodes.name: token_usage_cache.go
nodes.summary: Collects per-session AI token usage and cost metrics from session files, caching parsed summaries on disk to avoid reparsing unchanged files across refresh cycles.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an mtime+size cache key over JSONL session files with atomic write (temp file + rename) and 0o600 permissions.

nodes.name: CollectTokenUsageWithCache
nodes.summary: Glob-discovers session files, reuses cached summaries for unchanged files, reparses the rest, and aggregates token/cost metrics across all/today/7d/30d windows.

nodes.name: loadTokenUsageCache
nodes.summary: Reads and unmarshals the on-disk token usage cache file, returning an empty cache on absence or corruption.

nodes.name: saveTokenUsageCache
nodes.summary: Atomically persists the token usage cache to disk via a temp file, fsync-free rename, and 0o600 permissions.

nodes.name: parseTokenUsageFile
nodes.summary: Streams a JSONL session file line by line, tallying per-model token counts, daily buckets, and session cost/timing into a reusable summary record.

nodes.name: applyTokenUsageSummary
nodes.summary: Folds a single file summary into the global aggregation buckets, resolving model aliases and distinguishing subagent sessions across time windows.

nodes.name: resolveUsageModel
nodes.summary: Resolves a raw model identifier to its display name via the alias map, falling back to the canonical model name.

nodes.name: trim_label_test.go
nodes.summary: Table-driven unit test verifying TrimLabel strips trailing ID variants from session labels.

nodes.name: runtime_concurrent_seed_test.go
nodes.summary: Concurrency tests asserting CopyIfMissing is race-safe under parallel callers and CopyFile replaces destinations atomically without exposing partial reads.

nodes.name: runtime_failures_test.go
nodes.summary: Failure-path tests for CopyFile/CopyIfMissing covering read-only parents, unreadable sources, truncation, and concurrent-safe Homebrew runtime seeding with temp-file cleanup checks.

nodes.name: runtime_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for version detection, dashboard directory resolution, repo-root discovery, and Homebrew runtime seeding that preserves user config while updating versioned assets.

nodes.name: runtime.go
nodes.summary: Resolves dashboard/repo directories across repo-checkout and Homebrew layouts, seeds the Homebrew runtime dir with atomic file copies, and detects the OpenClaw version from file or CLI.
nodes.languageNotes: Atomic copies use a randomized temp sibling, fsync of file and parent dir, and hardlink-or-rename to guarantee crash-consistent replacement.

nodes.name: ResolveDashboardDirWithError
nodes.summary: Resolves the dashboard data directory from env override, repo-root discovery, or Homebrew runtime seeding, returning an error when none is usable.

nodes.name: findDashboardDir
nodes.summary: Walks upward from a starting directory looking for the refresh.sh marker that identifies the repo dashboard root.

nodes.name: SeedHomebrewRuntimeDir
nodes.summary: Populates the per-user Homebrew runtime directory by copying versioned assets while preserving existing user config, returning the resolved data dir.

nodes.name: CopyIfMissing
nodes.summary: Atomically copies a source file to a destination only if absent, using a temp sibling plus hardlink/rename so concurrent callers never observe a partial file.

nodes.name: CopyFile
nodes.summary: Atomically replaces a destination file with the source contents via a temp sibling, fsync, and rename.

nodes.name: openTempSibling
nodes.summary: Creates a uniquely named temp file next to the destination with the requested mode, retrying on collisions using crypto-random suffixes.

nodes.name: ResolveOpenclawPath
nodes.summary: Resolves the OpenClaw home path from an env override or the default user home location.

nodes.name: DetectVersion
nodes.summary: Detects the dashboard version from a VERSION file, falling back to a context-bounded git describe invocation that respects caller cancellation.

nodes.name: bench_test.go
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the server's loadData path for both cache-hit and cache-miss scenarios.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the Go 1.24+ b.Loop() benchmark idiom.

nodes.name: server_chat_handler_test.go
nodes.summary: End-to-end tests for the /chat handler covering disabled state, body/question size limits, bad JSON, rate limiting, missing data, and successful gateway round-trips against a stub server.

nodes.name: server_chat_handler.go
nodes.summary: HTTP handler for the AI chat endpoint: enforces rate limits and input bounds, builds the system prompt from cached dashboard data, and proxies the request to the AI gateway.

nodes.name: handleChat
nodes.summary: Validates the chat request (method, rate limit, body size, JSON, question length), loads cached data, builds the system prompt, and calls the AI gateway returning the answer or a mapped error status.

nodes.name: server_core_race_test.go
nodes.summary: Race-detector test asserting GetDataCached hands callers an independent map copy so concurrent reads and writes never collide.

nodes.name: server_core.go
nodes.summary: Defines the Server struct and its constructor, the per-IP chat rate limiter with background cleanup, and shared JSON response helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Rate limiter uses a sync.Map of token buckets with per-bucket mutexes and a ticker-driven eviction goroutine bound to the server context.

nodes.name: Server
nodes.summary: Central HTTP server holding config, rendered index HTML, cached dashboard data under RWMutex, the system service, and the chat rate limiter.

nodes.name: chatRateLimiter
nodes.summary: Per-IP token-bucket rate limiter backed by a sync.Map with allow and cleanup methods.

nodes.name: NewServer
nodes.summary: Constructs a Server: resolves the OpenClaw path, renders and escapes the index HTML with version metadata, wires the system service, and launches the rate-limiter cleanup goroutine.

nodes.name: allow
nodes.summary: Token-bucket check for a client IP that refills on a fixed interval and returns whether the chat request is permitted.

nodes.name: cleanup
nodes.summary: Evicts stale rate-limiter buckets whose last reset predates the retention cutoff.

nodes.name: sendJSONRaw
nodes.summary: Writes a raw JSON byte body with CORS, content-type, content-length headers and the given status code.

nodes.name: server_logs_pool_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests readMergedLogs against many and empty log sources, asserting correct merge counts and graceful handling of no input.

nodes.name: server_logs_saturation_test.go
nodes.summary: Test asserting the /errors handler reports a dropped-signatures count when the distinct-error map saturates its cap.

nodes.name: server_logs_test_helper_test.go
nodes.summary: Test helper wrapping apprefresh.ParseLogTimestamp so log timestamp parsing can be exercised from the appserver test suite.

nodes.name: server_logs_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for log source resolution and aliasing, timestamp/since parsing, merge-and-sort ordering across skewed sources, and the /logs and /errors handlers end to end.

nodes.name: server_logs.go
nodes.summary: Implements the /logs and /errors HTTP handlers: resolves and aliases log sources, merges tailed entries, filters by time window, and aggregates errors into deduplicated signature feeds.

nodes.name: handleLogs
nodes.summary: Parses limit/source/since query params, reads merged logs, filters by the since cutoff, and returns the bounded log entry list as JSON.

nodes.name: handleErrors
nodes.summary: Reads merged logs within an error window, groups occurrences by normalized signature into a capped feed, sorts by count or recency, and reports dropped signatures on saturation.

nodes.name: resolveSources
nodes.summary: Expands a raw comma-separated source query into concrete configured log sources, deduplicating and falling back to all configured sources.

nodes.name: resolveSourceToken
nodes.summary: Maps a single source alias token (e.g. gateway, bot) to matching configured source names via substring containment.

nodes.name: parseSince
nodes.summary: Parses the since parameter as either epoch milliseconds or an RFC3339 timestamp, falling back to a default when empty.

nodes.name: clampInt
nodes.summary: Parses an integer query value and clamps it to a default and min/max bounds.

nodes.name: server_refresh.go
nodes.summary: Manages the data refresh lifecycle: debounced background refresh with in-flight deduplication, mtime-based caching of data.json, and the /refresh handler that triggers and serves refreshed data.
nodes.languageNotes: Cache reads return maps.Clone copies under RWMutex so callers never share the live map with the writer.

nodes.name: startRefresh
nodes.summary: Starts a background refresh unless shutdown or one is already in flight, returning the in-flight channel for callers to await.

nodes.name: loadData
nodes.summary: Loads data.json with an mtime-keyed double-checked cache, parsing and storing both the raw bytes and a cloned map under the data mutex.

nodes.name: handleRefresh
nodes.summary: HTTP handler that triggers a debounced refresh, optionally waits for in-flight completion with a request-context timeout, and serves the freshest cached data.

nodes.name: GetDataCached
nodes.summary: Returns a cloned copy of the cached dashboard data map, loading it if necessary.

nodes.name: server_routes_symlink_test.go
nodes.summary: Security test asserting the static file handler rejects symlinks that resolve outside the web root.

nodes.name: server_routes.go
nodes.summary: Central HTTP router (ServeHTTP) dispatching to API and static handlers, with allowlisted static file serving guarded against path traversal/symlink escape, CORS headers, and the index/system endpoints.

nodes.name: ServeHTTP
nodes.summary: Routes incoming requests by path to the index, system, refresh, logs, errors, chat, or static-file handlers and enforces method restrictions.

nodes.name: HandleStaticFile
nodes.summary: Serves an allowlisted static asset, cleaning the path and resolving it within the web root to block traversal before writing the response.

nodes.name: readStaticFileWithinRoot
nodes.summary: Reads a static file only after evaluating symlinks and confirming the resolved target stays within the web root.

nodes.name: setCORSHeaders
nodes.summary: Applies CORS headers permitting loopback origins for cross-origin dashboard access.

nodes.name: handleSystem
nodes.summary: Serves the system metrics endpoint, returning cached system JSON or querying the system service with the request context.

nodes.name: server_test.go
nodes.summary: Core server tests covering static-file allowlisting and traversal, method restrictions, CORS (including IPv6 loopback), JSON send helpers, and the chat rate limiter allow/block behavior.

nodes.name: shutdown_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests refresh lifecycle behavior during shutdown: skipping new refreshes after context cancellation while still returning any in-flight completion channel.

nodes.name: atomic.go
nodes.summary: Provides crash-safe atomic file writes via a unique temp file, fsync, chmod, and rename for service unit/plist persistence.

nodes.name: writeFileAtomic
nodes.summary: Writes data to a temp sibling, fsyncs and closes it, applies the requested permission, then renames over the destination with cleanup on any failure.

nodes.name: uniqueTempPath
nodes.summary: Generates a non-colliding temp file path in a directory using the PID and an incrementing counter.

nodes.name: exec.go
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper running an external command with a default timeout and combined output capture for OS service management.

nodes.name: launchd_perm_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests permission and path-validation hardening of the launchd Install path: plist mode 0600, rejection of relative OPENCLAW_HOME, filtering of bad PATH entries, and idempotent reinstall.

nodes.name: launchd_tail_test.go
nodes.summary: Verifies tailFile sanitizes invalid UTF-8 in log lines so status output stays valid UTF-8.

nodes.name: launchd_test.go
nodes.summary: Comprehensive launchd backend test suite covering Install/Uninstall, lifecycle (start/stop/restart), status resolution, and plist/launchctl parsing helpers.

nodes.name: launchd.go
nodes.summary: macOS launchd service backend: renders the LaunchAgent plist, drives launchctl for install/uninstall/lifecycle, and resolves status (PID, port, uptime, log tail) by parsing plist XML and launchctl output.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses build-tagged platform backend with a streaming xml.Decoder for plist parsing and an injectable runCmd/probeFunc seam for testability.

nodes.name: probe.go
nodes.summary: HTTP liveness probe helper used by service backends to confirm the dashboard is actually serving on its bound port.

nodes.name: service_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the shared FormatStatus renderer and formatUptime helper for running/stopped states, empty log lines, and duration formatting.

nodes.name: service.go
nodes.summary: Cross-platform service contract: defines InstallConfig/ServiceStatus types, the Installer/Controller/Statuser/Backend interfaces, path-validation helpers, and human-readable status formatting.
nodes.languageNotes: Consumer-side interfaces (Installer/Controller/Statuser) composed into a Backend interface, following accept-interfaces-return-structs.

nodes.name: systemd_perm_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests systemd Install hardening: unit-file mode 0600, rejection of relative OPENCLAW_HOME, filtering of bad PATH entries, and idempotent reinstall.

nodes.name: systemd_test.go
nodes.summary: systemd backend test suite covering unit-file writing, path quoting, systemctl invocation, uninstall, lifecycle, status resolution, and unit/property parsing.

nodes.name: systemd.go
nodes.summary: Linux systemd (user) service backend: renders the unit file, drives systemctl --user for install/uninstall/lifecycle, tolerates benign systemctl failures, and resolves status via systemctl show plus journalctl log tail.
nodes.languageNotes: Build-tagged Linux backend with an injectable runCmd/probeFunc seam and a regexp-based unit-port parser.

nodes.name: unsupported.go
nodes.summary: Fallback service backend constructors for platforms without launchd or systemd, returning an unsupported-platform error.

nodes.name: bench_test.go
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the SystemService cache-hit GetJSON path and FormatBytes formatting using Go 1.24+ b.Loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses for b.Loop() {} (Go 1.24+) instead of the legacy b.N loop.

nodes.name: cold_path_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests the refresh cold-path deadline behavior: a hanging gateway must not exceed the bound, host metrics always ship, partial results are not cached, and degraded payloads are served and cached.

nodes.name: latest_version_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests getLatestVersionCached for race-free concurrent calls, returning cached value while refreshing, and negative caching of failures.

nodes.name: parse_proc_stat_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests parseProcStat across realistic /proc/stat samples, minimal fields, missing CPU line, optional steal field, and aggregate-vs-per-core selection.

nodes.name: system_collect_darwin_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests parseTopCPU on macOS top output: no-match error, integer idle, and fractional idle parsing.

nodes.name: system_collect_darwin.go
nodes.summary: macOS system-metrics collector: gathers CPU/RAM/swap concurrently by shelling out to top, sysctl, vm_stat, and sysctl swap, parsing their output into typed metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: Build-tagged darwin file using a sync.WaitGroup to fan out CPU/RAM/swap collection and compiled regexps for vm_stat parsing.

nodes.name: system_collect_linux_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests ramFromMeminfo and swapFromMeminfo for underflow safety, typical cases, and MemFree fallback when MemAvailable is missing.

nodes.name: system_collect_linux.go
nodes.summary: Linux system-metrics collector: samples /proc/stat twice for CPU usage and reads /proc/meminfo for RAM/swap, collecting CPU/RAM/swap concurrently with underflow-safe arithmetic.
nodes.languageNotes: Build-tagged linux file reading procfs directly; uses sync.WaitGroup fan-out and guards against unsigned underflow in meminfo math.

nodes.name: system_collect_unsupported.go
nodes.summary: Fallback metrics collectors for unsupported platforms, returning error-stamped CPU/RAM/swap values.

nodes.name: system_helpers_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests JSON coercion helpers (int64FromAny, stringSliceFromAny), parseOpenclawStatusJSON, and decodeJSONObjectFromOutput against varied inputs.

nodes.name: system_service_test.go
nodes.summary: Core SystemService test suite: byte formatting, type coercion, version comparison, JSON fetch body caps, gateway probing timeouts, atomic stale-decision concurrency, and hard-fail backoff.

nodes.name: system_service.go
nodes.summary: Central system-metrics service: caches and serves the system JSON payload, coordinates concurrent collection of CPU/RAM/swap/disk/versions/OpenClaw runtime with deadline bounds and hard-fail backoff, probes the OpenClaw gateway, and resolves the openclaw binary and latest npm version.
nodes.languageNotes: Large coordinator using sync.RWMutex caches, sync.Once binary resolution, context deadlines for cold-path bounding, and io.LimitReader to cap JSON response bodies.

nodes.name: system_types.go
nodes.summary: Data model for the system metrics API: the SystemResponse payload and its nested CPU/RAM/swap/disk/gateway/versions/OpenClaw structs with JSON tags.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure struct definitions with JSON tags and pointer Error fields for optional per-section error reporting.

nodes.name: extractPlistPath
nodes.summary: Test helper that extracts the PATH-env string from rendered plist XML for assertion.

nodes.name: NewWithContext
nodes.summary: Constructs a launchd backend bound to a context, resolving the per-user LaunchAgents plist directory.

nodes.name: Install
nodes.summary: Validates config paths, renders the plist atomically with hardened mode, and bootstraps the service via launchctl.

nodes.name: launchdOpenclawHome
nodes.summary: Resolves the OPENCLAW_HOME directory for the plist, rejecting relative paths and falling back to the user home.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Reports service status by parsing the plist for port/log path, querying launchctl for PID and uptime, probing HTTP, and tailing the log.

nodes.name: parseLaunchctlPID
nodes.summary: Extracts the PID from launchctl print output, tolerating reformatting and absent entries.

nodes.name: parsePlist
nodes.summary: Streams plist XML tokens to extract the bound port and log path independent of formatting.

nodes.name: tailFile
nodes.summary: Reads the last n lines of a file from a bounded tail window, sanitizing invalid UTF-8.

nodes.name: FormatStatus
nodes.summary: Renders a ServiceStatus into a human-readable multi-line status report including PID, uptime, port, and recent log lines.

nodes.name: formatUptime
nodes.summary: Formats a duration into a compact human-readable uptime string (e.g. 2h3m), warning on negative durations.

nodes.name: Backend
nodes.summary: Composite interface combining Installer, Controller, and Statuser that every platform service backend implements.

nodes.name: Install
nodes.summary: Validates paths, renders the systemd user unit atomically with hardened mode, and enables/starts it via systemctl.

nodes.name: Uninstall
nodes.summary: Stops and disables the unit, removes the unit file, and reloads the daemon, tolerating benign systemctl failures.

nodes.name: Status
nodes.summary: Resolves service status by parsing the unit port, querying systemctl show for PID/uptime, probing HTTP, and tailing journal logs.

nodes.name: isBenignSystemctlFailure
nodes.summary: Classifies systemctl errors that are safe to ignore during uninstall (e.g. unit already gone).

nodes.name: parseUnitPort
nodes.summary: Extracts the bound port from the unit ExecStart line using a compiled regexp.

nodes.name: collectCPU
nodes.summary: Collects macOS CPU usage by running top and parsing the idle percentage into a SystemCPU value.

nodes.name: collectRAM
nodes.summary: Collects macOS RAM usage from sysctl total memory and vm_stat page counts.

nodes.name: collectCPURAMSwapParallel
nodes.summary: Fans out CPU, RAM, and swap collection concurrently via a WaitGroup and returns the combined results.

nodes.name: parseTopCPU
nodes.summary: Parses the CPU-usage line from top output, computing busy percent from the idle figure.

nodes.name: parseVmStatUsed
nodes.summary: Computes used RAM bytes from vm_stat page-size and active/wired/compressed page counts.

nodes.name: parseSwapUsage
nodes.summary: Parses sysctl vm.swapusage output into total and used swap bytes.

nodes.name: collectCPU
nodes.summary: Samples /proc/stat twice with a short interval to compute Linux CPU busy percentage.

nodes.name: ramFromMeminfo
nodes.summary: Derives used/total RAM from parsed /proc/meminfo with underflow-safe arithmetic and MemFree fallback.

nodes.name: swapFromMeminfo
nodes.summary: Derives used/total swap from parsed /proc/meminfo, guarding against underflow when free exceeds total.

nodes.name: collectCPURAMSwapParallel
nodes.summary: Reads meminfo once and fans out CPU/RAM/swap derivation concurrently via a WaitGroup.

nodes.name: parseProcMeminfo
nodes.summary: Parses /proc/meminfo lines into a map of field name to kilobyte value.

nodes.name: parseProcStat
nodes.summary: Parses the aggregate cpu line of /proc/stat into idle and total jiffy counts, tolerating the optional steal field.

nodes.name: GetJSON
nodes.summary: Serves the cached system metrics JSON, deciding atomically whether the payload is fresh, stale, or needs a background/synchronous refresh under hard-fail backoff.

nodes.name: refresh
nodes.summary: Concurrently collects versions, OpenClaw runtime, disk, and CPU/RAM/swap under a cold-path deadline, assembles the SystemResponse, and caches both full and degraded payloads.

nodes.name: getVersionsCached
nodes.summary: Returns cached local versions or collects them under lock, poisoning-safe against deadline-cancelled partial results.

nodes.name: getLatestVersionCached
nodes.summary: Returns the cached latest npm version while triggering a single background refresh, with negative caching to suppress retry storms during outages.

nodes.name: CollectOpenclawRuntime
nodes.summary: Collects OpenClaw runtime state by concurrently probing gateway endpoints and parsing the openclaw status --json output, stamping freshness and aggregating errors.

nodes.name: probeOpenclawGatewayEndpoints
nodes.summary: Probes the OpenClaw gateway health and ready endpoints within a timeout, coercing the JSON responses into a typed gateway struct.

nodes.name: FetchJSONMap
nodes.summary: Issues a GET and decodes the response into a map, capping the body via io.LimitReader and rejecting non-200 statuses.

nodes.name: parseOpenclawStatusJSON
nodes.summary: Decodes openclaw status --json output into a SystemOpenclawStatus, merging in known version info.

nodes.name: decodeJSONObjectFromOutput
nodes.summary: Extracts and unmarshals the first JSON object embedded in noisy CLI output.

nodes.name: int64FromAny
nodes.summary: Coerces an arbitrary JSON-decoded value (int, float, json.Number) into an int64.

nodes.name: stringSliceFromAny
nodes.summary: Coerces an arbitrary JSON-decoded value into a string slice.

nodes.name: ParseGatewayStatusJSON
nodes.summary: Parses gateway status JSON into a SystemGateway value, resolving process info and classifying online/offline state.

nodes.name: FormatBytes
nodes.summary: Formats a byte count into a human-readable string scaled to B/KB/MB/GB.

nodes.name: GetProcessInfo
nodes.summary: Returns elapsed time and RSS for a PID by invoking ps, rejecting non-positive PIDs.

nodes.name: runWithTimeout
nodes.summary: Runs an external command under a derived timeout context, wrapping timeout and not-found failures with descriptive errors.

nodes.name: versionishGreater
nodes.summary: Compares two version-like strings token by token to determine ordering for binary selection.

nodes.name: ResolveOpenclawBin
nodes.summary: Locates the openclaw binary via PATH lookup or by scanning install directories and picking the highest version.

nodes.name: FetchLatestNpmVersion
nodes.summary: Fetches the latest published version from the npm registry within a timeout, returning empty on any failure.

nodes.name: SystemService
nodes.summary: Stateful metrics service holding RWMutex-guarded caches for the payload, versions, and latest version, plus config, shutdown context, and a once-resolved binary path.

nodes.name: SystemResponse
nodes.summary: Top-level system metrics API payload aggregating CPU/RAM/swap/disk, versions, OpenClaw runtime, thresholds, and freshness/error flags.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Application entry point that parses the command line, resolves runtime directories and version, enforces loopback-only HTTP binding, and dispatches service-management subcommands to the appservice backend.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-binary multi-command dispatcher; validateLoopbackBind gates non-loopback addresses behind an env opt-in.

nodes.name: Main
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestrator that wires flags, runtime/version resolution, the refresh collector, and the HTTP server, then handles service subcommands and graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: normaliseCmd
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes a service subcommand string into a recognized action, normalizing aliases and casing.

nodes.name: runServiceCmd
nodes.summary: Dispatches a normalized service subcommand to the matching install/uninstall/start/stop/restart/status action.

nodes.name: validateLoopbackBind
nodes.summary: Rejects non-loopback bind addresses at startup unless the OPENCLAW_DASHBOARD_ALLOW_NON_LOOPBACK opt-in is set.

nodes.name: localIP
nodes.summary: Resolves the host's primary local IP address for display or bind diagnostics.

nodes.name: main_shutdown_test.go
nodes.summary: Integration test that boots the binary, waits for it to listen, then verifies the graceful shutdown sequence completes cleanly.

nodes.name: TestShutdownSequence
nodes.summary: Spawns the server on a free port, waits for it to listen, signals shutdown, and asserts a clean exit.

nodes.name: server.go
nodes.summary: Root-package facade re-exporting the appserver HTTP server: thin wrappers for NewServer, ServeHTTP, PreWarm, and cached data accessors.
nodes.languageNotes: Root-package facade pattern: type alias Server plus wrapper functions delegating to internal/appserver.

nodes.name: Server
nodes.summary: Root-package alias/wrapper for the internal HTTP server type exposing the dashboard's request handling surface.

nodes.name: server_test.go
nodes.summary: Behavioral tests for the HTTP server facade covering routing, HEAD semantics, static-file safety, chat handler validation, CORS, rate limiting, cache coherence, and loopback bind enforcement.

nodes.name: system_service.go
nodes.summary: Root-package facade re-exporting appsystem helpers for system metrics collection, gateway status parsing, and OpenClaw runtime detection.
nodes.languageNotes: Facade wrappers delegating to internal/appsystem; NewSystemService is the only exported symbol.

nodes.name: system_test.go
nodes.summary: Extensive tests for the system metrics surface: /api/system handler, threshold clamping, gateway status JSON parsing, process info, byte formatting, OpenClaw binary resolution, and CORS behavior.

nodes.name: system_types.go
nodes.summary: Root-package type aliases re-exporting system metric structs from internal/appsystem for the facade surface.

nodes.name: refresh.go
nodes.summary: Root-package facade re-exporting apprefresh collectors and helpers: refresh runner, alerts, daily chart, cost breakdown, cron collection, and label formatting.

nodes.name: refresh_tokens.go
nodes.summary: Root-package facade re-exporting apprefresh token-usage helpers: token aggregation, bucket listing, formatting, and session-to-key mapping.

nodes.name: refresh_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for the refresh facade covering token-usage and subagent detection, alert building, cron parsing, cost breakdown, daily chart generation, and the refresh collector entrypoint.

nodes.name: service_cmd_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for service subcommand dispatch using a fake backend, verifying normaliseCmd parsing and that each action routes to the correct install/uninstall/start/stop/restart/status call.

nodes.name: version_test.go
nodes.summary: Tests for version detection and runtime directory resolution: VERSION file precedence over git tags, repo-root discovery, and Homebrew vs repo dashboard directory seeding.

nodes.name: VERSION
nodes.summary: Single-line semantic version string read at build/runtime to identify the dashboard release, taking precedence over git-tag fallback.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Build and quality-gate automation defining targets for build, test, lint, fmt, vet, staticcheck, govulncheck, coverage, and a composite check gate.
nodes.languageNotes: make check is the canonical gate chaining vet, lint, race tests, and govulncheck.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project documentation covering rationale, 12 dashboard panels, install paths (Homebrew, binary, one-line, Docker, Nix), service management, and theming.

nodes.name: TECHNICAL.md
nodes.summary: Deep internals reference documenting file structure, data pipeline, debounce and atomic-write mechanics, data sources, model-name normalization, token aggregation, and cost calculation.

nodes.name: 014-live-log-tail-error-feed.md
nodes.summary: TDD implementation plan for a live log tail and error feed panel, specifying gateway log formats, separate endpoints, and a backward-reading log tail reader.

nodes.name: RULES.md
nodes.summary: Codex engineering rules covering modern Go idioms, JSON/API design, naming, documentation, testing, I/O, concurrency safety, and lint expectations.

nodes.name: SKILLS.md
nodes.summary: Codex skills reference describing go-rig ATDD/TDD process, design discipline, dependency injection, package strategy, review checklist, and supporting review/frontend/ops skills.

nodes.name: TODO.md
nodes.summary: Bug tracker and fix log documenting resolved critical/medium/low issues with verification notes, including gateway probe, cache permissions, and timezone parsing fixes.

nodes.name: graphify-hook.sh
nodes.summary: Shell hook that keeps the graphify knowledge graph current after code changes.

nodes.name: uninstall.sh
nodes.summary: Uninstall script that removes the installed dashboard binary and associated service/runtime artifacts.

nodes.name: jobs.every.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture defining cron jobs with 'every'-style schedules used to exercise cron parsing in the refresh collector.

nodes.name: data-null.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture containing a JSON null payload to verify the server returns an error when the dashboard data file is null.

nodes.name: version.go
nodes.summary: Root-package facade exposing thin wrappers over appruntime for directory resolution and version detection, keeping the package public API stable while logic lives in internal/appruntime.
nodes.languageNotes: Root-package facade pattern: each function delegates one-to-one to an exported appruntime function, re-exporting internal APIs without adding behavior.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: The entire embedded single-page dashboard frontend: vanilla JS/CSS with no framework, comprising 12 collapsible metric panels (alerts, health, cost, charts, crons, sessions, usage, subagents, logs, errors, agent-config), SVG charts, a theme picker, and an AI chat, served to the browser via go:embed.
nodes.languageNotes: Single self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and vanilla JS organized into module-literal objects (Theme, State, DataLayer, Renderer, etc.); no build step, compiled into the Go binary via //go:embed.

nodes.name: Theme
nodes.summary: Theme module managing the color-scheme picker, persisting the selected theme and applying CSS custom properties to the dashboard.

nodes.name: State
nodes.summary: Central client-side state store holding the latest snapshot data fetched from the backend and exposing update helpers for the render layer.

nodes.name: DataLayer
nodes.summary: Data-fetching layer that polls the backend dashboard JSON endpoints and feeds results into State for rendering.

nodes.name: LogTail
nodes.summary: Diagnostics log-tail panel rendering streamed log entries with source and severity filtering across gateway, cron, session, and subagent sources.

nodes.name: ErrorFeed
nodes.summary: Error-feed panel that aggregates and renders recent error/warning entries with severity styling.

nodes.name: DirtyChecker
nodes.summary: Change-detection helper that diffs incoming snapshots against current state to avoid unnecessary re-renders of dashboard panels.

nodes.name: Renderer
nodes.summary: Core rendering engine that builds the agent tree, token tables, cost/model/subagent SVG charts, and the agent-config panel from snapshot data; the largest structural unit of the SPA.

nodes.name: Chat
nodes.summary: AI chat module handling user message input and exchange with the backend AI gateway, rendering the conversation in the dashboard.

nodes.name: SystemBar
nodes.summary: System-metrics bar rendering CPU, RAM, swap, and disk pills with warning/critical thresholds and a gateway-degraded indicator.

nodes.name: Sections
nodes.summary: Collapsible-sections controller that loads and persists per-panel collapse state and wires up expand/collapse toggles.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Application bootstrap that initializes all dashboard modules and starts the periodic data refresh and render loop.

nodes.name: OCUI
nodes.summary: Top-level OCUI namespace tying together the dashboard modules and exposing the public UI entry surface invoked on page load.

layers.name: Entry Point & Bootstrap
layers.description: The cmd binary entry, root Main dispatcher, version facade, and bootstrap that resolves runtime dirs, enforces loopback binding, and routes service subcommands.

layers.name: Presentation (Web SPA)
layers.description: The embedded vanilla-JS single-page dashboard frontend with its 12 collapsible metric panels, plus the runtime theme and config assets shipped to style it.

layers.name: HTTP & Server
layers.description: The appserver HTTP router, static-file serving, chat/logs/refresh handlers, caching, and rate limiting, plus the root server facade that re-exports it.

layers.name: Data Collection (Refresh)
layers.description: The apprefresh collector pipeline — sessions, tokens, crons, gateway/git probes, log tailing, daily aggregation, and caching — plus root refresh facades and cron test fixtures.

layers.name: System Metrics
layers.description: The appsystem service that caches and serves the system JSON payload, gathers CPU/RAM/swap/disk per platform, probes versions and OpenClaw runtime, plus root system facades.

layers.name: OS Service Management
layers.description: The appservice package managing launchd/systemd install, lifecycle, and status with atomic unit-file writes, liveness probes, and an unsupported-platform fallback.

layers.name: Chat & AI Gateway
layers.description: The appchat package that builds the AI system prompt from dashboard metrics and calls the AI gateway over HTTP with token redaction, plus the root chat facade.

layers.name: Config & Runtime
layers.description: The appconfig config structs, JSON/dotenv loading and validation, and appruntime directory resolution and version detection, with root config facade and example configs.

layers.name: Build, Infrastructure & CI/CD
layers.description: Container, Nix, GoReleaser, and Make build definitions, install/uninstall scripts, and GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows that test, validate, and release the dashboard.

layers.name: Documentation & Agent Tooling
layers.description: Project docs, architecture/technical references, changelog, implementation plans, and the Claude/Codex/Gemini/OpenCode agent rules, skills, and graphify tooling configs.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what OpenClaw Dashboard is: a beautiful, zero-dependency command center that collects everything about your OpenClaw AI agents — gateway health, costs, cron status, active sessions, sub-agent runs, model usage, and git log — into a single local page with no login and no cloud. ARCHITECTURE.md complements it with the repo layout, runtime model, and package boundaries you will explore in the steps ahead. Together they frame the core design choice: stdlib-only Go serving an embedded single-page app.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The root main.go is where everything begins: it parses the command line, resolves runtime directories and the version, enforces loopback-only HTTP binding for safety, and dispatches service-management subcommands. The thin cmd/openclaw-dashboard/main.go wrapper simply delegates to dashboard.Main and translates its return into an os.Exit code, keeping the binary trivial. This is the bird's-eye view of how the dashboard boots and which subsystems it wires together.
tour.languageLesson: Go binaries live under cmd/<binary>/main.go while real logic stays in the root package and internal/ packages. The cmd entry stays minimal so the same Main function can be unit-tested in-process without spawning a subprocess.

tour.title: Configuration & Runtime Resolution
tour.description: Before serving anything, the dashboard must know where it lives and how it is configured. appruntime resolves the dashboard and repo directories across both repo-checkout and Homebrew layouts, seeds the Homebrew runtime dir with atomic file copies, and detects the OpenClaw version. appconfig defines the configuration structs and loads them from JSON with unknown-key warnings, threshold clamping, .env parsing, and home-directory expansion — and as the highest fan-in file in the codebase, config.go is depended on by nearly every package.
tour.languageLesson: Config loading clamps out-of-range values and warns (via slog) on unknown keys rather than failing hard — a forgiving-input, strict-validation pattern. Secrets and tunables come from JSON or env, never hardcoded.

tour.title: The HTTP Server & Routing
tour.description: With config and runtime resolved, the server takes over. server_core.go defines the Server struct, its constructor, the per-IP chat rate limiter, and shared JSON response helpers. server_routes.go is the central ServeHTTP router that dispatches to API and static handlers, serves the embedded SPA with allowlisted static files guarded against path-traversal and symlink escape, and sets CORS headers. The root server.go facade re-exports these so callers like main.go get a stable public API.
tour.languageLesson: This is the root-package facade pattern: logic lives in internal/app<domain>/ and the root dashboard package holds thin wrappers and type aliases. It keeps the public surface stable while letting internals evolve.

tour.title: Serving the Embedded SPA
tour.description: The entire frontend is a single web/index.html — vanilla JS and CSS with no framework — comprising the 12 collapsible metric panels described in the README (alerts, health, cost, charts, crons, sessions, tokens, sub-agents, models, skills, git, and AI chat). It is compiled directly into the Go binary via go:embed, which is why frontend changes require a rebuild. The runtime themes.json supplies the palettes (midnight, nord, catppuccin, and more) that style it.
tour.languageLesson: Go's //go:embed directive bakes static assets into the compiled binary at build time, so the dashboard ships as one self-contained executable with no separate web-asset deployment.

tour.title: The Data Refresh Pipeline
tour.description: This is the heart of the dashboard. refresh.go orchestrates the data-collection pipeline: it fans out concurrent collectors, parses config, aggregates sessions and token usage, and assembles the data.json payload the SPA renders. The root refresh.go facade re-exports the collector runner, alerts, daily chart, cost breakdown, and cron helpers, while TECHNICAL.md documents the debounce, atomic-write, and aggregation mechanics behind it.
tour.languageLesson: The collector fans work out across goroutines and joins the results, but each goroutine has a clear shutdown path and context-bounded timeouts — concurrency used only where it measurably helps.

tour.title: Collectors: Sessions, Tokens & Crons
tour.description: Building on the pipeline from Step 6, these files do the actual gathering. refresh_sessions.go loads per-agent session stores, derives group names, resolves session models, and backfills channel connectivity. refresh_tokens.go (with its disk-cached token_usage_cache.go) defines token buckets and aggregates per-model usage and cost across time windows. refresh_crons.go collects cron job state, supporting both legacy-inline and split-store sidecar layouts. The shared refresh_helpers.go — second-highest fan-in in the package — provides the typed JSON accessors they all lean on.

tour.title: Alerts, Charts & Log Tailing
tour.description: The collector also turns raw data into the dashboard's higher-level signals. refresh_alerts.go builds alerts from cost, cron, context, gateway, and memory thresholds and produces a sorted cost breakdown; refresh_chart.go merges fresh daily aggregates with persisted history into the 30-day series. logtail.go is a self-contained engine that tails multiple log sources, merges the latest records via a heap, parses JSONL/plain lines, classifies severity, and normalizes error signatures — powering the live log and error-feed panels.

tour.title: System Metrics
tour.description: The System Health panel is backed by appsystem. system_service.go is the central service that caches and serves the system JSON payload, coordinating concurrent collection of CPU, RAM, swap, disk, versions, and OpenClaw runtime status, with platform-specific collectors shelling out to top/sysctl on macOS and reading /proc on Linux. system_types.go defines the SystemResponse data model — a high fan-in struct shared by every collector. The root system_service.go facade re-exports it for the server.
tour.languageLesson: System metrics use a cold-path deadline: a hanging gateway probe must never block the response, host metrics always ship, and partial results are not cached — graceful degradation under a hard time bound.

tour.title: Chat & the AI Gateway
tour.description: The AI Chat panel lets you ask questions about your dashboard. appchat's chat.go builds an AI system prompt from the current dashboard metrics and calls the AI gateway over HTTP, with bearer-token redaction and structured GatewayError handling. The server_chat_handler.go wires this into an HTTP endpoint that enforces rate limits and input bounds, builds the prompt from cached data, and proxies to the gateway. The root chat.go facade keeps the public API thin.
tour.languageLesson: Errors are wrapped with %w and bearer tokens are scrubbed from error messages before they surface — context flows in, internal secrets never flow out.

tour.title: OS Service Management
tour.description: The dashboard can install itself as a background OS service, dispatched from main.go in Step 2. appservice's service.go defines the cross-platform contract — InstallConfig/ServiceStatus types, the Backend interface, and the shared FormatStatus renderer. launchd.go and systemd.go implement that contract for macOS and Linux respectively, handling install, lifecycle, and status resolution, while atomic.go provides crash-safe atomic writes for the unit/plist files and unsupported.go is the fallback for other platforms.
tour.languageLesson: This is consumer-side interface design: one small Backend interface with launchd and systemd implementations selected per platform, so the dispatch logic stays platform-agnostic. Unit/plist files are written atomically (temp file, fsync, rename) to survive crashes.

tour.title: Containerization & Deployment
tour.description: The Dockerfile packages the dashboard into a production image using a multi-stage build: the golang:1.26-alpine builder stage compiles a CGO-free static binary and copies web assets, while the alpine:3.23 runtime stage copies only the binary plus runtime assets, runs as a non-root user, exposes port 8080, and adds a healthcheck. flake.nix provides the equivalent reproducible Nix build, both honoring the project's CGO_ENABLED=0 constraint.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds separate the heavy build toolchain from the slim runtime image; only the compiled artifact crosses the stage boundary, keeping the final image small and free of compilers.

tour.title: CI/CD & Release
tour.description: As a capstone, see how code gets tested and shipped. The tests.yml workflow runs the Go suite plus vet, lint, and vulnerability checks; pr-validate.yml gates pull requests against the golangci-lint config (gosec + errorlint enabled). On a tag, release.yml invokes GoReleaser (.goreleaser.yml) to build cross-platform binaries, generate SBOMs, sign artifacts, and publish the Homebrew tap. The Makefile's check target is the canonical local gate mirroring this pipeline.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions use 'on' triggers and 'jobs' for parallelism; concurrency groups cancel superseded runs. Release automation is tag-driven, turning a single git tag into signed, multi-platform artifacts.
